tests.description: Two-arm parallel where one arm interrupts and one runs to completion. The completed arm increments a module-level `counter` global as a side effect. After resume, the test asserts counter is exactly 1 — if the BranchState.result caching is broken and the completed arm re-executes on resume, counter would be 2 (or more) and the exact-match would fail. This addresses the gap described in fork/multi-cycle/partial-cycle-cached: pure non-interrupting branches don't reveal re-execution, but a side-effecting branch does.
